headline: Transcript: Britain — a beacon of economic stability?
summary: George Parker talks to Miranda Green, Stephen Bush and Chris Giles
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the Political Fix podcast episode: ‘Britain — a beacon of economic stability?’

[MUSIC PLAYING]

George ParkerConference season is over and that leaves one big thing looming over the political calendar. Yes, it’s the Autumn-ish Budget and the countdown is on. Chancellor Rachel Reeves has just six weeks to fill the gaping fiscal gap before the big day on November 26th. It was a week jam packed with back-to-back meetings and budgetary acronyms. The IFS released its Green Budget. The ONS published its beige figure for August GDP. And rumours swirled about a potential productivity downgrade from the OBR. For the most part, Reeves was in Washington for the annual IMF meetings, waving the flag for Britain as a beacon of stability, while pinning some blame for stagnation on her party’s rivals.

Rachel Reeves voice clipAusterity, Brexit and the ongoing impact of Liz Truss’s mini Budget, all of those things have weighed heavily on the UK economy.

George ParkerSo
